tech.title: Sony ends production of the cassette Walkman
tech.description: Sony officially ends production of its cassette-based Walkman in most markets, closing the chapter on one of the most iconic consumer electronics products ever made.

tech.title: Wikipedia’s English edition surpasses 500,000 articles
tech.description: Wikipedia’s English-language edition passes half a million articles, demonstrating the rapid growth of user-generated online knowledge.

tech.title: AT&T breakup officially takes effect
tech.description: The U.S. government-mandated breakup of AT&T takes effect, ending decades of monopoly control over American telecommunications.

tech.title: ARPANET switches to TCP/IP
tech.description: ARPANET adopts the TCP/IP protocol suite, allowing networks to communicate through a shared standard and laying the foundation for the modern internet.

history.title: World Trade Organization comes into existence
history.description: The World Trade Organization officially begins operations, establishing a global framework for regulating international trade.

history.title: David Dinkins sworn in as New York City’s first Black mayor
history.description: David Dinkins is inaugurated as mayor of New York City, becoming the first African American to hold the office.

history.title: Republic of China is established
history.description: The Republic of China is proclaimed following the collapse of the Qing dynasty, ending over two thousand years of imperial rule.

history.title: City of Greater New York is created
history.description: New York City consolidates surrounding municipalities to form the modern five-borough structure still used today.

history.title: Emancipation Proclamation takes effect
history.description: President Abraham Lincoln’s Emancipation Proclamation goes into effect, declaring freedom for enslaved people in Confederate-held territory.

world_events.title: Deadly New Year’s Eve bar fire in Crans-Montana, Switzerland
world_events.description: A fire during New Year’s Eve celebrations in Crans-Montana kills at least 40 people and injures more than 100 others.

world_events.title: Vehicle-ramming and shooting attack in New Orleans
world_events.description: A vehicle-ramming and shooting attack during New Year celebrations in New Orleans kills 14 people and injures dozens more.

world_events.title: Major earthquake strikes western Japan
world_events.description: A powerful earthquake strikes western Japan, killing hundreds and injuring over a thousand people.

world_events.title: Steamboat Willie and original Mickey Mouse enter the public domain
world_events.description: Disney’s copyright on Steamboat Willie expires, allowing the earliest depiction of Mickey Mouse to enter the public domain.

world_events.title: Deadly stadium stampede in Abidjan, Ivory Coast
world_events.description: At least 60 people are killed and more than 200 injured in a stampede following New Year celebrations at a stadium in Abidjan.

crime_and_justice.title: Nightclub attack in Istanbul kills dozens
crime_and_justice.description: An attacker opens fire at a nightclub in Istanbul during New Year celebrations, killing dozens and injuring many others.

crime_and_justice.title: Bangkok nightclub fire kills 66 people
crime_and_justice.description: A fire at a Bangkok nightclub during New Year celebrations kills 66 people, becoming one of Thailand’s deadliest nightclub disasters.

crime_and_justice.title: Alcatraz becomes a federal prison
crime_and_justice.description: Alcatraz Island officially begins operating as a United States federal prison in San Francisco Bay.

war_and_conflict.title: NAFTA takes effect
war_and_conflict.description: The North American Free Trade Agreement enters into force between the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

war_and_conflict.title: Cuban Revolution succeeds
war_and_conflict.description: Fulgencio Batista flees Cuba as Fidel Castro’s revolutionary forces take power, reshaping Cold War geopolitics.

sports.title: NHL Winter Classic continues New Year’s Day tradition
sports.description: The NHL Winter Classic remains a marquee New Year’s Day sporting event, drawing large crowds and national television audiences.

sports.title: First Rose Bowl game is played
sports.description: The first Rose Bowl game is held in Pasadena, launching one of college football’s most famous traditions.

science.title: NASA’s New Horizons nears Pluto ahead of historic flyby
science.description: NASA’s New Horizons spacecraft enters its final approach phase toward Pluto, preparing for its first-ever close encounter later in the year.

disasters.title: Eastern Air Lines Flight 980 crashes in Bolivia
disasters.description: Eastern Air Lines Flight 980 crashes into Mount Illimani in Bolivia, killing all 29 people aboard.

disasters.title: Air India Flight 855 crashes into the Arabian Sea
disasters.description: Air India Flight 855 crashes shortly after takeoff from Bombay due to instrument failure, killing all 213 people on board.

births.title: Verne Troyer is born
births.description: Actor Verne Troyer is born, later becoming widely known for his role as Mini-Me in the Austin Powers film series.

births.title: Bob Menendez is born
births.description: Bob Menendez is born, later becoming a U.S. Senator representing New Jersey and a prominent figure in American politics.

births.title: Gary Johnson is born
births.description: Gary Johnson is born, later serving as the 29th Governor of New Mexico and running for president as a Libertarian candidate.

births.title: Christine Lagarde is born
births.description: Christine Lagarde is born, later becoming a leading global economic figure and head of major international financial institutions.

births.title: E. M. Forster is born
births.description: English novelist E. M. Forster is born, later writing influential works exploring class, empire, and social change.

deaths.title: Mario Cuomo dies
deaths.description: Mario Cuomo, former Governor of New York and a major figure in American liberal politics, dies at age 82.

deaths.title: Patti Page dies
deaths.description: Singer and actress Patti Page, one of the best-selling female vocalists of the 1950s, dies at age 85.

deaths.title: Grace Hopper dies
deaths.description: Computer scientist and U.S. Navy rear admiral Grace Hopper dies, leaving behind a legacy that includes co-developing the COBOL programming language.

deaths.title: Hank Williams dies
deaths.description: Country music legend Hank Williams dies at age 29, leaving a lasting impact on American music and songwriting.
